LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS GEOFFREY PALERMO, JUSTICE INVESTORS AND PORTSMOUTH SQUARE, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING THIS ACTION - THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS DENIED. PARAGRAPHS 6.4 AND 6.5 (DISPUTE RESOLUTION PROVISIONS) OF THE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP INTEREST REDEMPTION OPTION AGREEMENT ("OPTION AGREEMENT") APPLY TO THIS CASE. PLAINTIFF'S TORT CLAIMS IMPLICATE "INTERPRETATION OR ENFORCEMENT OF" THE OPTION AGREEMENT. THE COURT NEEDS TO REFER TO THE OPTION AGREEMENT TO DETERMINE THE PARTIES' OBLIGATIONS AND THOSE OBLIGATIONS WILL HELP DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN TORTIOUS CONDUCT. THE TERMS OF THE DISPUTE RESOLUTION CLAUSES ARE BROAD ENOUGH TO ENCOMPASS TORT AND CONTRACT CLAIMS. SEE BUCKHORN V. ST. JUDE HERITAGE MED. GROUP (2004) 121 CAL.APP.4TH 1401. IN ADDITION, EVEN IF DEFENDANTS HAD THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, DEFENDANTS WAIVED THAT RIGHT. DEFENDANTS ACTED INCONSISTENTLY WITH THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND PLAINTIFF IS PREJUDICED. PLAINTIFF SUBSTANTIVELY RESPONDED TO DEFENDANTS' DISCOVERY AND EXPENDED RESOURCES ADDRESSING THE PARTIES' DISCOVERY DISPUTES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT MOORE & ATTY. MICHAEL J. BETZ OF ALLEN MATKINS, TEL. NO. 273-7465 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JESSE L. MILLER OF REED SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 659-5973 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ATTY. SEAN M. SELEGUE OF ARNOLD & PORTER LLP, TEL. NO. 471-3169 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GEOFFREY PALERMO, JUSTICE INVESTORS AND PORTSMOUTH SQUARE, INC.'s Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying This Action - The motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. Paragraphs 6.4 and 6.5 (dispute resolution provisions) of the Limited Partnership Interest Redemption Option Agreement ("option agreement") apply to this case. Plaintiff's tort claims implicate "interpretation or enforcement of" the option agreement. The court needs to refer to the option agreement to determine the parties' obligations and those obligations will help determine whether defendants engaged in tortious conduct. The terms of the dispute resolution clauses are broad enough to encompass tort and contract claims. See Buckhorn v. St. Jude Heritage Med. Group (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1401. In addition, even if defendants had the right to compel arbitration, defendants waived that right. Defendants acted inconsistently with the right to compel arbitration and plaintiff is prejudiced. Plaintiff substantively responded to defendants' discovery and expended resources addressing the parties' discovery disputes. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
